title: Planchonella menait
intro: Planchonella menait, also known as Menai plum, is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to the tropical rainforests of Australia and New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Planchonella menait include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Planchonella menait include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
